title: Grammitis urceolaris
taxonomy: Grammitis urceolaris is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Grammitis, which is a group of epiphytic ferns commonly found in tropical regions. The family Polypodiaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, often referred to as the polypody family. Within the family, Grammitis urceolaris is part of the order Polypodiales, which also includes other types of ferns with similar characteristics. The classification of Grammitis urceolaris reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in its family and order.

intro: Grammitis urceolaris, also known as the Urceolate Grammitis or the Urceolate Rockcap, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, characterized by its small, round rosettes of fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and inflammatory conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of G. urceolaris include various flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or decoction, allowing its bioactive compounds to be extracted and utilized for their therapeutic benefits.
